diagrams.title: Separate Chaining
diagrams.description: A visual representation of separate chaining collision resolution where each hash table bucket contains a linked list of all keys that hash to that index. Shows the hash table array on the left and linked list nodes branching from each bucket containing collisions.
elements: Linked list nodes showing key-value pairs
diagrams.purpose: Explains how separate chaining handles collisions by maintaining a linked list at each bucket, allowing multiple elements to share the same hash index

diagrams.title: Linear Probing
diagrams.description: Demonstrates linear probing collision resolution where collisions are resolved by sequentially checking the next available slot in the array. Shows a hash table with keys being inserted and the probing sequence path.
elements: Probe sequence arrows showing collision resolution
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates linear probing where each collision is resolved by moving to the next consecutive slot until an empty position is found, demonstrating the primary clustering problem

diagrams.title: Double Hashing
diagrams.description: Shows double hashing collision resolution using two hash functions to compute the probe sequence. Demonstrates how the secondary hash function provides different step sizes to avoid clustering.
elements: Primary hash function h1(key) = key mod 13
Probe sequence calculation: index = (h1 + i * h2) mod 13
Multiple keys showing different probe paths
diagrams.purpose: Explains double hashing where two independent hash functions determine the probe sequence, reducing primary and secondary clustering compared to linear and quadratic probing
